articles.description: Nifty below 23,400 level; metal shares shine  Business Standard

topStories.title: Epstein urged media mogul to give up control of affairs, citing health
topStories.description: Late sex offender urged US-Canadian billionaire Mortimer Zuckerman to enter guardianship or conservatorship, files show.

topStories.title: Iran negotiating with FIFA to move World Cup 2026 matches from US to Mexico
topStories.description: The national football federation wants to relocate its group matches to Mexico due to the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran.

topStories.title: NIA court remands 7 foreigners, including Ukrainians and US citizen, to 11-day custody
topStories.description: Special NIA Court at Patiala House Court remanded 6 foreigners for 11 days of National Investigation Agency (NIA) custody.

topStories.title: Iranian Drones Target US Embassy, Hotel In Baghdad. Oilfield Also Attacked
topStories.description: The US Embassy and a hotel in Baghdad were targeted by drones early Tuesday, with video appearing to show air defences engaging a projectile near the embassy, CNN reported.

topStories.description: The Supreme Court has held that the Air Force Group Insurance Society (AFGIS) is a 'State' under Article 12 of the Constitution as it performed public duty by protecting and looking after the welfare...

topStories.title: Venezuela defeats Italy, sets up WBC final against US
topStories.description: Venezuela is through to its first World Baseball Classic final and will play the host nation for the title in Florida.

topStories.title: South African fruit exports face delays as Iran war disrupts shipping
topStories.description: South Africa's fruit exporters face mounting losses and disruption as the US-Israel war on Iran enters its fourth week.

topStories.description: Cuba also says it is removing impediments to US businesses and other foreign investors.

topStories.description: The Iranian retaliation, now in its 18th day, has decreased in intensity but remains a threat to Gulf nations and Israel.

topStories.description: Cuba’s national power grid has collapsed amid an ongoing US-imposed oil blockade, causing blackouts across the country.

dailyNews.title: Top Democrat in US House slams Trump’s ‘reckless war of choice’
dailyNews.description: Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries criticised Donald Trump for what he called a "reckless war on choice" on Iran".

dailyNews.title: Trump asks if Venezuela could join US as its 51st state: ‘Statehood…anyone?’
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday wondered out loud whether Venezuela could become the 51st state of the United States.

dailyNews.title: Israel policy looms large over US elections in Illinois amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Pro-Israel groups pour millions into Democratic primaries in Chicago area as support for US ally hits a historic low.

dailyNews.title: Haryana Rajya Sabha poll: BJP, Congress win 1 seat each in midnight cliffhanger | In numbers
dailyNews.description: BJP's Sanjay Bhatia and Karamvir Boudh of the Congress were declared winners by the returning officer for the two Haryana Rajya Sabha seats.

dailyNews.title: Palestinian activist Leqaa Kordia released from ICE detention
dailyNews.description: Palestinian activist Leqaa Kordia was released from an ICE detention facility in Texas after a year in custody.

dailyNews.title: Live: Trump says US shocked as Iran strikes Gulf countries
dailyNews.description: Live Trump says US shocked as Iran strikes Gulf countries

dailyNews.title: Trump says presidents 'should not have learning disabilities' as he mocks Newsom's dyslexia
dailyNews.description: The National Center for Learning Disabilities said it was "disturbed" by the president's remarks.

dailyNews.title: Attempts to open Strait of Hormuz by force would be ‘almost suicidal’
dailyNews.description: Andreas Krieg says that US plans to deploy 2,500 marines to the Gulf is unlikely to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Minnesota bill would ban warrants allowing police to collect data from devices near a crime scene
dailyNews.description: Minnesota lawmakers proposed a bill to ban reverse location warrants allowing police to collect cellphone data near crime scenes, which they say violates the Fourth Amendment.

dailyNews.title: Venezuela comes from behind to beat Italy, earn WBC final spot vs Team USA
dailyNews.description: Venezuela staged a dramatic comeback to defeat Italy, 4-2, at loanDepot park, advancing to World Baseball Classic final against Team USA on Tuesday night.

dailyNews.title: Jasmine Crockett defends her security guard who was killed in police standoff, wanted for impersonating cop
dailyNews.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett's security guard killed in a SWAT standoff was accused of impersonating police, and the congresswoman defended him despite his lengthy rap sheet.

dailyNews.title: MLB upholds 80-game suspension for Phillies' Johan Rojas after appeal
dailyNews.description: Phillies outfielder Johan Rojas will serve his 80-game MLB suspension despite an appeal after testing positive for a performance-enhancing drug.

topStories.title: Irish Prime Minister Taoiseach Micheál Martin addresses how Europe feels about US-Iran conflict
topStories.description: Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin discusses European perceptions of U.S.-Iran conflict ahead of Trump meeting, advocating for diplomatic solutions over military action.

topStories.title: Jane Fonda slams Paramount-WBD merger, warns CNN will 'have to cave' to Trump
topStories.description: Actress Jane Fonda protested the Paramount merger at an Oscars after-party, warning about the Trump administration's potential political influence over CNN.

topStories.title: Trump questions Newsom’s fitness for White House, citing his dyslexia
topStories.description: After Gov. Gavin Newsom admitted to having a 960 SAT score and dyslexia, Trump questioned his fitness for office and accused him of talking down to Black audiences.

topStories.title: Trump lawyer in Jack Smith case draws conservative backing after DOJ praise rattles 'elite' legal conference
topStories.description: John Lauro told a conference that the DOJ was "in a better place" under Trump, prompting criticism from conference attendees.

topStories.title: Europe Rejects Trump’s Demands for Warships to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: While some European countries said they were discussing ways to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, several rejected President Trump’s calls to send warships.

topStories.title: What Are Trump’s Positions on Iran? They Can Change by the Sentence.
topStories.description: The president is no stranger to staking out contradictory stands, part of what his aides say is his negotiating style. But on Iran, his shifting positions are colliding with the consequences of war.

topStories.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
topStories.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.

dailyNews.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
dailyNews.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.

dailyNews.title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
dailyNews.description: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.

dailyNews.title: Oil Begins Flowing Through California Pipeline Under Trump Order
dailyNews.description: Oil had not flowed through the pipeline since a 2015 rupture caused an environmental disaster on California’s Central Coast. It sets up a new fight between the Trump administration and state officials.

dailyNews.title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
dailyNews.description: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.

dailyNews.title: Judge Strikes Down RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policies in Blow to Trump’s Health Agenda
dailyNews.description: Ruling on a lawsuit brought by several prominent medical organizations, a district court said the federal government had not based its decisions on science in limiting Covid shots and revising the childhood immunization schedule.

dailyNews.title: Richard Grenell: From Trump Cabinet Hopeful to Ex-Kennedy Center Manager
dailyNews.description: Richard Grenell once hoped to be President Trump’s secretary of state. Instead, Mr. Trump just replaced him as Kennedy Center president.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
dailyNews.description: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with President Trump’s bluster.

dailyNews.title: How Trump Destroyed the Aura of the Wartime President
dailyNews.description: Even as he’s wrecking American institutions, he is revealing the limits of his cultural influence.

dailyNews.title: Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz paralyzed and gas prices on the rise
dailyNews.description: The Iran war could escalate further as President Trump threatens to hit key oil infrastructure if Tehran doesn't drop its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: 3/9: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," after a whiplash week of on-again, off-again tariff announcements, Canadian ambassador to the U.S. Kirsten Hillman joins to discuss the costs and consequences of a trade war. Plus, former White House Russia expert Fiona Hill discusses President Trump's diplomatic pivot on Ukraine.

dailyNews.title: Alleged pipe bomber argues charges should be tossed under Jan. 6 pardons
dailyNews.description: A man who was accused of planting pipe bombs outside the RNC and DNC on the eve of the Jan. 6 attack is arguing he is covered by President Trump's sweeping pardons of alleged Jan. 6 rioters.

dailyNews.title: 3/16: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Trump calls for other countries to help open Strait of Hormuz; White House chief of staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with breast cancer.
